text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data instead of being explicitly programmed. In supervised learning, the computer is given training data that includes both input examples and correct answers. For example, you might show a computer thousands of photos of cats and dogs, each labelled correctly. The computer uses this data to build a model, which is a mathematical system that recognises patterns. Once trained, the model can make predictions on new, unseen images. After making predictions, we measure the model’s accuracy by comparing its results to the correct answers. A more accurate model is better at making reliable predictions in real-life situations.
